title: Meshless  Structure-Preserving  Algorithms
text:
Course overview


Part I: Theory - From Lagrangian to Eulerian coordinates via Euler-Poincaré reduction

Variational principles vs. Newtonian mechanics
Variational principles in fluid mechanics (field theories)
Lagrangian vs. Eulerian descriptions of fluid motion
Euler-Poincaré reduction





Part II: Discretization - Particle methods for fluid Lagrangians

Functions (0-forms) and volume forms
Discrete Lagrangians
Smoothed-particle hydrodynamics (SPH)





Part III: Software - Solving meshfree discretizations with Struphy

Struphy: an open-source Python package for solving PDEs
Hands-on session: Implementing and running SPH simulations with Struphy

title: Meshless  Structure-Preserving  Algorithms
text:
Key ideas of Part I


Variational principles vs. Newtonian mechanics

\[
\frac{\mathrm d}{\mathrm dt} \frac{\partial L}{\partial \dot x} - \frac{\partial L}{\partial x} = 0\quad \Leftrightarrow \quad m \ddot x = -\frac{\partial V}{\partial x}
\]



Variational principles in fluid mechanics (field theories)

\[
\mathcal A(\mathbf u, \rho, s) = \int_0^T \int \left( \frac{|\mathbf u|^2}{2} - \mathcal U(\rho, s) \right) \rho\, d\mathbf x\, dt\,,\qquad \delta \mathcal A = ?
\]
\(\mathbf u (t,\mathbf x)\) fluid velocity, \(\rho(t,\mathbf x)\) mass density, \(s(t,\mathbf x)\) entropy density



Lagrangian vs. Eulerian descriptions of fluid motion

\[
\frac{\mathrm d}{\mathrm dt} \mathbf x(t, \mathbf x_0) = \mathbf u(t, \mathbf x(t, \mathbf x_0))\,,\qquad \mathbf x(0, \mathbf x_0) = \mathbf x_0 \quad \Leftrightarrow \quad
\left\{
  \begin{aligned}
    &\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \rho + \nabla \cdot (\rho \mathbf u) = 0
    \\
   &\frac{\partial}{\partial t} \mathbf u + (\mathbf u \cdot \nabla) \mathbf u = - \frac{1}{\rho}\nabla V
  \end{aligned}
\right.
\]



Euler-Poincaré reduction

\[
\mathcal A(\mathbf x, \dot{\mathbf x}) \Leftrightarrow \mathcal A(\mathbf u, \rho, s)
\]

title: Meshless  Structure-Preserving  Algorithms
text:
Key ideas of Part II


Functions (0-forms) and volume forms

\[
\rho: \mathbf x \in \Omega \mapsto \rho(\mathbf x) \in \mathbb R,\qquad \rho^V: \Omega \subset_\textrm{open} \mathbb R^3 \to \mathbb R,\quad \int_\Omega \rho^V(t, \mathbf x) \,\mathrm d \mathbf x
\]



Discrete Lagrangians

\[
\rho(t, \mathbf x) \approx \rho_h(t, \mathbf x) = \sum_{i=1}^N w_i \, \delta(\mathbf x - \mathbf x_i(t)) \quad \Rightarrow \quad \mathcal A(\mathbf u, \rho, s) \approx \mathcal A_h(\mathbf x_1, \ldots, \mathbf x_N, \dot{\mathbf x}_1, \ldots, \dot{\mathbf x}_N)
\]



Smoothed-particle hydrodynamics (SPH)

\[
A(\mathbf x) = \int A(\mathbf y) \delta(\mathbf x - \mathbf y) \, d\mathbf y \approx \int A(\mathbf y) W(\mathbf x - \mathbf y, h) \, d\mathbf y \approx \sum_{i=1}^N w_i A(\mathbf x_i) W(\mathbf x - \mathbf x_i, h)
\]

title: Meshless  Structure-Preserving  Algorithms
text:
Key ideas of Part III


Struphy: an open-source Python package for solving PDEs



FAIR software: Freely available, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable
Available on GitHub (https://github.com/struphy-hub/struphy) and PyPI
Built on top of Pyccel for high-performance code generation




Hands-on session: Implementing and running SPH simulations with Struphy


Setting up a simple SPH simulation of the Euler equations
Exploring the Struphy API and documentation
Running and visualizing results with Struphy’s built-in tools

Try installing Struphy according to the instructions in the README before the hands-on session.
